Topics today:
Historically great copy – primary solutions, hot seats and Chiasmas
5:30
Homework hot seats – what is working and what is not
Always send to the niche and not the quick set up – this way Jon can view the processes.
Tim G – Back pain course
Tims writing is short, they can be expanded out – it is ok to be lengthy in these sections.
Read through the copy out loud in the proofs section to make sure the words are right.
False solutions section
· 	Give people reasons why
· 	Elaborate the ideas
· 	There needs to be several here
· 	Tell a story is the goal and learning how to sell it is important
7:45
Myths section
The way that the myth is explained matters
Give more of a story in this section as well
Tim’s example -
Can usually expect only a “symptoms relief” solution
Jons example
In an email you can use something like this:
If you rely on the healthcare establishment solution to solve your back pain problems, you can
usually just expect only a system relief. There is a better idea… go here now.
9:00
Primary solution
Back pain blueprint sounds like a book
There needs to be something inside the blueprint – it has to be a catchy name
30 Day Final solution was used – there may be historical ties to a phrase like this.
There needs to be a unique mechanism to grab the attention of the reader (what is different that
what everyone else is doing with a unique twist):
Could it be:
· 	Stretches

interesting

Interesting teaching
Primary solution tip –
This doesn’t sound as cool – start here and expand – use this to get a quick start or an idea to

When all else fails, go literal and when that fails us the holly grail.
14:00
Is “Something I call” cant be used then..
The trick to it all was a secret tea with cayenne pepper for the flat belly fix
The trick to it all was a secret __________ (item) with _________ (item) for the _________
name of product
Holy grail example
The _________ (primary solution) is what I call the holy grail of ________ (niche)
This is used to get the mind going
Mother natures cure for __________ (problem)
Technical difficulties 15:00 – 16:50
Name the entry question – name them all different in the false solution – this makes finding them
easy for later use or editing.
False solutions – Tim had his too short – Elaine had hers too long
Elaine
Write more in the truth – these are not meant to be short
Primary solution
The save your brain protocol – sounds like a book
Click on rules, examples and video to make sure the path is correct
The primary solution is not ever the name of the product
The mechanism – there is something about the product that is special
Elaine’s example – it’s a sequence of steps to see why your brain isnt working and what you can
do about it
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Jon’s example – My product contains a technique called “Neuro Filtering”, it is a 10 step
process where we ask you 10 specific questions that re-train your brain into thinking
__________ (example)
It is the unique mechanism that makes the name of the product work
Jon came up with the name “Neuro Fingerprints” or “Neuro Signature”
__________ (Name of product) is powered by __________ (unique mechanism) this is only
found within __________
__________ (Name of product) is special because __________ (unique mechanism) this is only
found within __________
26:00 – name ideas
Jon walks the class though how he came up with the name “Neuro signature solution”
Jon went through and thought of fingerprints because they are unique
Brain is related to neuro
Unique to X – what is unique – fingerprints are unique
Elaine came up with signature
Primary solution names
This is how to come up with primary solutions names that are interesting –
What is it doing __________ (description)
What is it related to __________ (make lists and then modify with a thesaurus)
28:45 – Story and offer structure tips
Take my avatars and turn them into reluctant heroes (“my wife ran into the room in tears…) This
allows the prospect to see themselves in the story. And assuming I am the “working hero” the
rest of my audience will lock onto my journey
There is power in starting the in the middle of the story – The wife in the above example is a
reluctant hero – the husband is the hero for finding solution. In this example I am talking to the
avatar and the avatar target (someone who the avatar may share the example with – someone
doing weight loss would share their success with their family)
(not in class) ==== (This middle of the story could be at the height of the emotional arch – it
would have to be something interesting enough to grab the attention of the buyer – think of the
process like Quinton Tarantino movies. This can go the other direction and start with a highly
successful moment – I was standing on the stage in front of 10,000 people accepting an award)
30:30 - Chiasmus
Tell the story in a chiasmus. This is a Grecian story narrative found throughout many Greek
legends (even in the bible)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 8 --

Turn history into a story
This isnt difficult to understand but it can be hard to do without knowing how.
The Chiasmus formula
1. 	Point 1
2. 	Point 2
3. 	Point 3 (the crux of the story)
4. 	Invert point 2
5. 	Invert point 1
This is a point counterpoint structure
There is a version of a chiasmus
1. 	Point 1 – The worst thing you can do to lose fat is cardio
2. 	Point 2 – Cardio drains your energy, and your body needs that energy to repair muscle
damage after a workout
3. 	Point 3 (the crux of the story) In fact, muscle growth and resistance training is a far better
long term fat burring solution
4. Invert Point 1 – Cardio is great for your heart, and you should do mild forms of it like brisk
walking, which actually helps restore energy (duality / push pull).
5. Invert point 2 – The best advice for increasing your metabolism and burning fat is to
exchange cardio for resistance training.
This example would be longer in all reality
In a chiasmus the ideas in the formula are opposite points
Point 1 - Worst thing 	Point 5 - Best advice (this is a persuasive sandwich)
Point 1 - Lose fat 	Point 5 - Increase metabolism
Point 1 - Cardio 	Point 5 - Resistance training
There is a guy that breaks down he bible chapters of Mathew or Mark to show what has been
done.
Stories and offers can use a Chiasmus
X can be alright.. .however
The brain locks onto an implanted idea.
In traditional copywriting this is called tying down
The worst thing you can do is cardio, that’s why and now you know the worst thing you can do
is cardio.
The worst thing you can do is __________ (action) and that’s why you know the absolute worst
thing for you to do is __________ (action) (think in terms of reason why) When anchored down
the next step is simple…
When speaking of __________ (new action)(now there is a transition)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

Remember you also need _________ (item)
This is how to build a syllogistic case for the reader.
36:15
Flat belly fix – this is a top offer on Clickbank right now
“Discover how I helped my “crippled” wife shed 23 pounds of unwanted fat and completely
flatten her belly. (without starving herself and without doing any exercise more strenuous than
walking to the fridge)
The pattern should leap out – action / without (specific numbers)
Facebook cringes with burn or lose
Here is the headline formula
· 	Discover _________ (something positive about your product)
· 	Specific item about that help the avatar in a way that sticks out like a sore thumb
· 	Without __________ (a difficult task – this makes it easy for buy in or even something
that is unbelievable – a claim that gets more attention)
Copy form the page
At first I didn’t want to tell you my wife’s story…
It felt too private, Too painful, however
The near-miraculous health and weight loss trick we stumbled on that literally saved my wife’s
life and gave her back her learn, sexy and pain free body is something too important to hide.
Now the story starts here:
She woke up screaming
That’s what I remember the most.
That’s what broke my heart
Everything up to this point from the class is in this letter. All of the techniques have been used.
The flatbellyfix.com/written/ - go through this letter and look for all of the items we have learned
so far in the HEX (tricks / tactics)
The writer has made his wife into a hero
When Jon reads copy he looks for purpose in each sentence – this is why a chiasmus works so
well to build a case for the reader / product.
Look for ways to put something juicy into each sentence
· 	Beautiful bad ass
· 	Take down a perp
· 	Meanest power forward on basketball team
· 	Shut up anyone with a cop stare
· 	Woke up in a cold sweat
· 	Jaw clenched tight
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

· 	Screamed so loud
These items put the reader into the story
Women read this and love it – the audience is 70% women.
They want to relate to a man that loves a woman
Dream come true + nightmare (duality formula)
43:40 - Unique mechanism
2 weeks later the solution hit me in the face (pepper spray)
The hotter your body the easier to burn fat
Cayenne pepper (unique mechanism)
The holy grail spice that saved my wife’s life
46:00 – Questions and answers
Judy – Look for - What are the things about us that identify us as unique (find ways to talk to the
avatar)
Dovi – Chiasmus in sales letters – is the sales letter structured as one long chiasmus or short
versions in the section – Answer – Long form is difficult – short sections are easier and good
reminders to tie information down for the reader. Jon says this may work well in blind emails.
Tie information with a go back and read that dfirecction (get them to go back and read the
chiasmus)
Dovi – Please elaborate about a reluctant hero – Answer – this is a form of the hero’s journey –
life thrust me into a situation and now this is how I have fixed it or I was forced to act
Abraham Lincoln was a reluctant hero – the circumstances made him the hero he was.
Every copywriter should know and understand the hero’s journey
A heart attack survivor that needed to learn about heart disease and then conquered it is a
reluctant hero.
Tip – when stuck to come up with a story use an avatar where they are the reluctant hero. Using
something personal is always the best.
Great examples are:
· 	Star wars – Luke
· 	Harry Potter – Harry
· 	Alice in wonderland – Larry
I was minding my own business when _________ (problem) happened
There are plenty of books on the heroes journey – 16 steps (this is a chiasmus by the way)
Stand up comedy does a similar version of a chiasmus – this makes the audience pay attention
through recognition and makes people feel smart
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 8 --

The last sentence in a chiasmus tie down makes people feel smart.
54:00
Heather Havenwoods Las Vegas event with Chirs Haddad, Jon Carlton and Joe Sugarman
John Carlton – You want to make the person reading your letter feel like a genius – we both
know and then tie it down. Here is something that will help you with your golf swing …
something called ________ . This item does __________ and ________ , now you know how to
do this because __________ helps your golf swing
Trivia questions at a movie theatre
This creates dopamine rushes in the viewer / reader
Look for ways to get yeses and dopamine
Facebook is all about the dopamine hits
Chiasmus are used in
· 	Music
· 	Comedy
· 	Copy
Fractals in art can cause people to see fractals everywhere
The brain wants to key in on things and fill in the blanks (we look for patterns)
The brain is a pattern recognition machine – some copy leaves blanks to fill in
The book is always better than the movie is because when reading the mind fills in the blanks
58:40
Judy – who dissected the chiasmus in the New Testament – Dr Richard Carrier
Tim – 30 day final solution was not the real name – It should have been the 30 day final answer
Does that name tell people this is a back pain solution? If there were not pictures on the book
could people know what the gist of the book was
How to title books
Look at the most popular search terms on the internet
Hollis Carter & Michael Lovich (Baby Bathwater Mastermind)
Hollis helped people through amazon by naming the books by the search terms
#1 search term on google or amazon (word for word) this will cause people to click on the book
A title for a book should allow for people not to guess or think about
· 	Fit over 40
· 	Every other day diet
The primary solution may still be mysterious
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

Get people to think _ “I don’t know what it is .. but I want it”
The title should be self explanatory
Make offers that people cant refuse
Look up how offers are structured in Sellerator – not every offer works this way, a call to action
needs to be compelling and easy to understand
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
